title: Nephrodium lacerum
intro: Nephrodium lacerum, also known as Lace fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The health benefits of Nephrodium lacerum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Nephrodium lacerum is often used to make tea, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including bronchitis, asthma, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Nephrodium lacerum is classified as a species of extinct fern within the family Matoniaaceae. It belongs to the division Pteridophyta, which encompasses various types of vascular plants. This fern is a part of the phylum Tracheophyta and is grouped under the kingdom Plantae. Nephrodium lacerum was a fossilized species, and its classification is based on its fossilized remains. Further details about this fern's classification can be found in paleobotanical studies.

The plant contains compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. Some studies suggest that extracts from Nephrodium lacerum may have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it a potential natural remedy for wound healing and skin infections.
